top_findings.summary: The skill requests unrestricted Bash command execution (Bash(*)) and WebFetch capabilities, which are excessive for its stated purpose of production scheduling. While file operations (Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep) are reasonable for reading job data and writing schedule outputs, arbitrary Bash access and network requests pose significant security risks. These permissions could be exploited to run malicious commands, exfiltrate sensitive data, or perform unrelated actions far beyond the scope of scheduling algorithms, without clear justification in the skill description.
